title: E2E Human-Friendly Feedback System
topic: PLA-0020 — message constants, toast layer, mmff-cli, hints
summary: Architecture for centralised error messages, global toast notifications, power-user CLI, and contextual hints.
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Overview
  2. 2. Key Findings
  3. 3. Current State — Error Surface Audit
  4. 4. Architecture & Design Decisions
  5. 5. Wire Format & Message Constants
  6. 6. Frontend: Toast Layer (Sonner)
  7. 7. mmff-cli: Power-User Access
  8. 8. Hints Substrate (Phase 2)
  9. 9. Gaps & Next Steps
  10. 10. Sources

1. Overview

Vector's error and feedback surface today is fragmented: backend returns machine-oriented strings like \"invalid credentials\" and \"invalid id\" visible directly to users; the frontend has no global toast layer (all feedback is scattered across 15+ local useState instances); success states are completely silent; and tips/hints have no delivery mechanism. This paper documents the E2E feedback system that closes these gaps: centralised human-readable message constants in Go, consistent RFC 9457 wire format across all endpoints including middleware, a global Sonner toast layer in React, a power-user CLI for authenticated API access, and a hints substrate for contextual onboarding.

2. Key Findings

  • Message constants belong in a single file. Backend/internal/messages/messages.go — all human-readable strings (auth, request, generic, success) in one place. Easier to review for tone, completeness, and i18n readiness. Cost: one rebuild to change a string; benefit: never again hunt for where an error message lives.
  • Middleware must emit RFC 9457. Today 14 call sites in security/csrf.go, auth/middleware.go, and realtime/client.go use http.Error() with plain text. These bypass httperr and return non-standard shapes. Migrating them to httperr.Write() gives consistent {\"type\", \"title\", \"status\", \"detail\", \"instance\"} JSON across the entire surface.
  • Frontend has no notification bus. Every component that fails does const [error, setError] = useState() and renders locally. A global Sonner toast layer (one `<Toaster />` in root layout, one notify.* helper) centralises success/error/info/hint feedback and eliminates repetition across 15+ call sites.
  • Sonner is the right choice. Zero dependencies, SSR-safe (Next.js App Router ready), renders outside React tree (no z-index fighting), MIT licensed, minimal footprint. Alternatives (react-toastify, react-hot-toast) carry more deps or have SSR friction.
  • Success feedback is missing. Mutations are fire-and-forget — users get no confirmation. Adding `notify.success('Saved.')` on logout, create, delete, and save operations closes the feedback loop and builds user confidence.
  • A CLI multiplies developer velocity. Power users and CI pipelines benefit from an authenticated command-line tool. A compiled Go binary at backend/cmd/cli/ (using Cobra + Viper) keeps the API surface testable without a browser, stores sessions in ~/.mmff/session.json, and avoids curl boilerplate for routine operations.
  • Hints need localStorage gating. Contextual tips ('Drag rows to reorder', 'Archiving removes from active views') should fire once and suppress on repeat via localStorage keys. Phase 2 work; Phase 1 is the toast substrate.

3. Current State — Error Surface Audit

Backend error landscape (summary):

CategorySitesHTTP StatusCurrent Messages
Unauthorized18+401\"unauthorized\", \"invalid credentials\", \"no refresh token\", \"token expired\"
Forbidden9403\"forbidden\", \"password_change_required\" (ad-hoc JSON)
Bad Request — Invalid ID46400\"invalid id\", \"bad id\", \"invalid addressable id\"
Bad Request — Invalid Body35400\"invalid request body\", \"bad request\"
Not Found45+404\"not found\", service-layer err.Error()
Conflict8409\"duplicate label\", \"cap reached\", err.Error()
Internal Error95+500\"internal error\"
CSRF (non-httperr)2403\"csrf token missing\", \"csrf token mismatch\" (plain text)
Auth Middleware (non-httperr)9+401/403\"unauthorized\", \"forbidden\", {\"error\":\"password_change_required\"} (plain text or ad-hoc JSON)

Frontend error display patterns:

  • <div className=\"form__error\">{message}</div> — inline below forms (library-releases/page.tsx, ArchiveMapFlyout.tsx, Table.tsx)
  • <span className=\"profile-bar__error\" role=\"alert\"> — inline beside profile bar (ProfileBar.tsx)
  • <span className=\"pop-up-change-banner__error\"> — banner inside popover (preferences/navigation/page.tsx)
  • All error strings are local state; no shared error context or bus
  • Success feedback: none — mutations update silently

4. Architecture & Design Decisions

4.1 Centralised Message Constants

Decision: Single Go constants file at backend/internal/messages/messages.go, grouped by semantic category (Auth, Request, Generic, Success).

Rationale:

  • All strings in one place makes tone review, completeness audits, and future i18n straightforward
  • No more hunting through 400+ handler files for where a message lives
  • Go compiler ensures no typos or escaping errors
  • Cost (rebuild to change string) is negligible for a backend service

Structure:

package messages

const (
  AuthInvalidCredentials = \"The email or password you entered is incorrect.\"
  AuthAccountLocked      = \"Your account has been locked after too many failed attempts. Please contact your administrator.\"
  AuthUnauthorized       = \"You must be signed in to do that.\"
  // ... 15+ more
)

const (
  RequestInvalidBody     = \"The request body was not valid.\"
  RequestInvalidID       = \"The ID provided was not valid.\"
  // ... more
)

4.2 RFC 9457 Wire Format (Consistent)

Decision: All error responses (including middleware) emit the same JSON shape: {type, title, status, detail, instance}.

Current Gaps:

  • 14 call sites in security/csrf.go, auth/middleware.go, and realtime/client.go use http.Error() with plain text or ad-hoc JSON
  • Two defects/handler.go sites use httperr.Write() but pass err.Error() as detail instead of calling httperr.WriteValidation()

Fix: Migrate all http.Error() calls to httperr.Write(w, r, status, messages.*). This guarantees:

  • Content-Type: application/problem+json for all errors
  • Consistent detail field (human-readable)
  • Frontend receives predictable shape for error parsing

4.3 Global Toast Notification Layer

Decision: Use Sonner (npm package) mounted once in app/layout.tsx. Single app/lib/toast.ts helper exports notify = { success, error, info, hint, apiError }.

Why Sonner:

  • Zero dependencies (single ESM file ~6KB gzipped)
  • SSR-safe: renders outside React tree via portal, no hydration mismatch
  • Next.js App Router compatible (no getInitialProps or _app workarounds)
  • MIT licensed, actively maintained
  • Minimal API surface — no complex state management needed

Topology:

app/components/Toaster.tsx:
  export function Toaster() {
    return <Sonner position=\"bottom-right\" theme=\"system\" />;
  }

app/lib/toast.ts:
  export const notify = {
    success: (msg: string) => toast.success(msg),
    error: (msg: string) => toast.error(msg),
    info: (msg: string) => toast.info(msg),
    hint: (msg: string) => toast(msg),
    apiError: (err: ApiError, fallback?: string) =>
      toast.error(err.detail ?? err.message ?? fallback),
  };

app/layout.tsx:
  <>
    <Toaster />
    {children}
  </>

4.4 Power-User CLI (mmff-cli)

Decision: Compiled Go binary at backend/cmd/cli/ using Cobra (CLI framework) + Viper (config/env vars). Stores tokens in ~/.mmff/session.json, reads MMFF_API_URL env var (default localhost:5100).

Why CLI over curl wrapper:

  • Type-safe (Go compiler catches flag mismatches)
  • Shell completion (bash, zsh, fish) — tab-complete resource names
  • Structured output (JSON or formatted tables)
  • Session management — login once, token stored, auto-refresh on 401
  • Portable — single binary, no dependency on bash/curl versions
  • Future: can add import/export, batch operations, watch-for-changes

Phase 1 scope (WS4-A): Auth subcommands (login, logout, me, refresh) + shared HTTP client

Phase 2 scope (WS4-B): Resource groups (portfolio-models, portfolio-items, roles, work-items, sprints)

4.5 Hints Substrate (Phase 2)

Decision: app/lib/hints.ts constants file (mirrors messages.go pattern), localStorage gating to suppress repeats.

Usage: notify.hint(Hints.DragToReorder) on first user interaction with a feature; localStorage key \"hint_shown_DragToReorder\" suppresses future fires.

Examples:

  • 'Drag rows to reorder.' — fired on first mouse enter on draggable row
  • 'Archiving removes from active views but keeps history.' — fired on first open of archive flyout
  • 'Use @ to mention team members.' — fired on first focus of a text input with @ mention support

5. Wire Format & Message Constants

Backend Message Groups (backend/internal/messages/messages.go):

GroupConstsExample
Auth10AuthInvalidCredentials, AuthAccountLocked, AuthTokenExpired, AuthUnauthorized, AuthForbidden, AuthPasswordChangeRequired, AuthCSRFInvalid, AuthInvalidCurrentPassword
Request4RequestInvalidBody, RequestInvalidID, RequestMissingFields, RequestBadRequest
Generic4NotFound, Conflict, InternalError, ServiceUnavailable
Success2PasswordChanged, PasswordResetSent

Wire shape (httperr.Write output):

POST /v1/api/auth/login (bad credentials):
{
  \"type\": \"about:blank\",
  \"title\": \"Unauthorized\",
  \"status\": 401,
  \"detail\": \"The email or password you entered is incorrect.\",
  \"instance\": \"/v1/api/auth/login\"
}

POST /v1/api/auth/me (no token):
{
  \"type\": \"about:blank\",
  \"title\": \"Unauthorized\",
  \"status\": 401,
  \"detail\": \"You must be signed in to do that.\",
  \"instance\": \"/v1/api/auth/me\"
}

6. Frontend: Toast Layer (Sonner)

Installation: npm install sonner

Usage patterns:

// Success feedback after mutation:
const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
  try {
    await api('DELETE', `/v1/api/portfolio-items/${id}`, {});
    notify.success('Item deleted.');
    // Refetch list
  } catch (err) {
    if (err instanceof ApiError) {
      notify.apiError(err);
    }
  }
};

// Error feedback (automatic):
try {
  const res = await api('/v1/api/auth/login', { email, password });
} catch (err) {
  notify.apiError(err, 'Login failed.');
}

// Info/hint:
notify.info('Changes will be saved automatically.');
notify.hint(Hints.DragToReorder);

Styling: Sonner toasts inherit CSS variables from root (--color-*, --font-family, etc.). No custom CSS per toast — the renderer applies theme automatically.

7. mmff-cli: Power-User Access

Build: go build -o mmff-cli ./backend/cmd/cli

Phase 1 (WS4-A) surface:

$ mmff-cli auth login --email user@mmffdev.com --password secret
✓ Logged in as user@mmffdev.com
  Token stored in ~/.mmff/session.json
  Expires: 2026-05-06T15:34:10Z

$ mmff-cli auth me
ID:          583b8276-092f-4645-8e79-367fdcb5c4b6
Email:       user@mmffdev.com
Role:        User
Permissions: portfolio.list, portfolio_items.view
Active:      true

$ mmff-cli auth logout
✓ Logged out

Phase 2 (WS4-B) surface (sketch):

$ mmff-cli portfolio-models list
ID                                  Title              Created
00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001 Default Model      2026-05-01

$ mmff-cli portfolio-models get 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001 --json
{\"id\":\"...\", \"title\":\"Default Model\", ...}

$ mmff-cli roles list
Code        Label    Rank  System
gadmin      Gadmin   20    true
padmin      Padmin   15    true
user        User     10    true

Architecture (backend/cmd/cli/):

  • main.go — Cobra root, version flag, global config (MMFF_API_URL)
  • cmd/auth.go — login, logout, me, refresh subcommands
  • client/client.go — shared HTTP client (reads token, sets headers, handles 401 + refresh retry, formats output)
  • client/session.go — read/write ~/.mmff/session.json (stores access_token, refresh_token, expires_at)

8. Hints Substrate (Phase 2)

What: Contextual onboarding tips triggered on first interaction, localStorage-gated to prevent repetition.

File: app/lib/hints.ts

export const Hints = {
  DragToReorder: 'Drag rows to reorder.',
  ArchiveToRemove: 'Archiving removes from active views but keeps history.',
  MentionTeamMembers: 'Use @ to mention team members.',
  // ... more
} as const;

Integration: On relevant user interaction, call:

const maybeShowHint = (hint: string) => {
  const key = `hint_shown_${hint}`;
  if (!localStorage.getItem(key)) {
    notify.hint(Hints[hint]);
    localStorage.setItem(key, 'true');
  }
};

// On first drag of a row:
onDragStart={() => maybeShowHint('DragToReorder')}

9. Gaps & Next Steps

  • ~400 more httperr.Write call sites need message constants. WS1-B is a batch mechanical update across all handler packages. No semantic changes, just string replacement.
  • Component toast migration (WS2-B) is incremental. ProfileBar.tsx, ArchiveMapFlyout.tsx, topology handlers, library-releases/page.tsx, preferences/navigation/page.tsx — replace useState error with notify.apiError().
  • CLI resource coverage (WS4-B) grows as needed. Start with auth + portfolio-models/items/roles; extend to work-items, sprints, custom-fields later.
  • i18n ready but not implemented. Message constants are strings, easy to hand off to Crowdin or similar. No code changes needed when i18n lands — just swap the Go constants.
  • No API key generation in Phase 1. CLI uses JWT Bearer token (same as frontend). If API key auth is needed later, the wire format (RFC 9457) is already set and won't require frontend changes.

10. Sources

  1. RFC 9457 — Problem Details for HTTP APIs
  2. Sonner — Toast library for React
  3. Cobra — Go CLI framework
  4. Viper — Configuration library for Go
  5. Go net/http — standard library
  6. backend/internal/httperr/httperr.go — existing RFC 9457 implementation
  7. app/lib/api.ts — existing ApiError class and error parsing
